mission.title: flowise: [BUG] REST API upsert new document - all the time a new document store is created
mission.description: [BUG] REST API upsert new document - all the time a new document store is created. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify flowise troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your flowise deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flowise -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flowise
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flowise -n flowise --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review flowise configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant flowise configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n flowise -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flowise
kubectl get configmap -n flowise -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=flowise
```
I'm using the python code present in 
https://docs.flowiseai.com/using-flowise/document-stores#id-10.-api
Want to reproduce:  
**Scenario 1: In the same document store, use an existing document loader configuration, upsert as new document

steps.title: Apply the fix for [BUG] REST API upsert new document - all the time a new…
steps.description: Hi and , Please follow step by step guide: 1. POST `{{current_url}}/api/v1/document-store/store` ```json { "name": "Create from API", "description": "Create from API" } ``` This will create Document Store, remember to record down the `id`, as shown in screenshots:

steps.title: Confirm [BUG] REST API upsert new document - all the time… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flowise -n flowise --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n flowise --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: Hi and , Please follow step by step guide: 1. POST `{{current_url}}/api/v1/document-store/store` ```json { "name": "Create from API", "description": "Create from API" } ``` This will create Document Store, remember to record down the `id`, as shown in screenshots:

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with flowise installed or the issue environment reproducible.
